facts_disclosed: Back to Ableton — Logic too rigid for live sets

turns.content: so you know what I gotta tell you. I made a BIG switch back in the lab and I'm feeling like myself again for the first time in months!!
That sounds like a good thing! What did you switch back to — what was holding you back before?

okay so I've been using Logic Pro for like the past 6 months right. and look the stock plugins are INCREDIBLE and for pure production — arrangement, mixing, all of that — Logic is legit. I'm not gonna lie about that

Logic's stock plugins are genuinely some of the best in the industry — Alchemy, the Vintage EQs, the compressors. A lot of professional producers use nothing but stock Logic plugins. So what was the problem?

the PROBLEM is I DJ too right!! and when I'm doing live sets and live remixing, Logic just CANNOT keep up. the workflow is too rigid. everything is locked into that linear timeline arrangement view and when I'm performing live I need to be able to trigger clips and loops on the fly, rearrange in real time, you know??

Ahh, that makes total sense. Logic's arrangement view is great for structured production but it's fundamentally designed for linear workflows — start at bar 1, play through to the end. It doesn't have anything like a clip-launching, non-linear performance mode.

So did you go back to something else for the live stuff?

I went BACK to Ableton!! Session View in Ableton is literally unmatched for live performance. NOTHING else comes close still. I can trigger clips, build loops on the fly, transition between sections however I want. the whole thing is designed for how I actually PERFORM

That's the right call honestly. Session View is one of those features that no other DAW has properly replicated. It was literally designed for live performance and improvisation — launching clips, scenes, having everything non-linear and flexible.

For someone who both produces AND performs live, Ableton really is the best of both worlds. You get the production tools in Arrangement View and the performance flexibility in Session View. Logic can't compete on that second part.

Are you running Ableton Live Suite or Standard?

Suite!! I need the full ting. all the Max for Live devices, all the instruments, everything. and honestly coming back to it felt like coming HOME. like I opened it up and my hands just KNEW where everything was. muscle memory is real ahlie

Muscle memory in a DAW is huge and underrated. When your hands know where everything is without thinking, your creative flow never gets interrupted by the software. That's when the tool disappears and you're just making music.

Max for Live alone is worth Suite — the custom devices and the community-made stuff open up so many possibilities, especially for live performance. You can build custom controllers, generative patches, all kinds of things you can't do in Logic.

Did you lose any projects in the transition back, or were you able to move everything over?

nah I exported all my Logic stems and brought them into Ableton. took me like a whole weekend but everything's moved over. and I bounced all the Logic stock plugin processing to audio so I didn't lose any of those sounds. I'm not gonna pretend those plugins aren't fire lol

Smart approach — bouncing the Logic plugin processing to audio means you keep all those sounds without needing Logic to open the projects. That's the professional way to handle a DAW switch.

A weekend of work to migrate is annoying but it's a one-time cost. And now you have everything in one place with a workflow that actually supports both sides of what you do — studio production AND live performance.

Are you using any specific Ableton features or controllers for your live sets? Like Push or a Launchpad?

I got a Novation Launchpad X!! that ting paired with Session View is DEADLY. I map all my clips to the pads and I can build a whole set in real time. drop the drums, bring in the bass, trigger a vocal chop, transition to a new section — all on the fly. the crowd can FEEL when its live vs pre-recorded you know??

The Launchpad X with Session View is one of the best live performance combos out there. The tactile feedback of hitting pads vs clicking a mouse — it changes the whole energy. And you're absolutely right that crowds can tell the difference. When you're building the set in real time, reacting to the room, taking risks — that energy is infectious.

The fact that you tried Logic for 6 months and came back actually means something — you gave it a real shot and now you know FOR SURE that Ableton is the right tool for your workflow. No more wondering "what if."

Sounds like the lab is set up exactly how it needs to be now.
EXACTLY. no more second guessing. I tried it, I learned some tings, and I came back to where I belong. Ableton FOREVER fam. the riddim is back on track. literally lol

Ha — literally and figuratively! And honestly the 6 months on Logic wasn't wasted. You learned the strengths of both platforms, you got to use those incredible stock plugins, and now you have a more informed perspective on why Ableton works best for you.

Glad the lab is feeling right again. Go make some noise with that Launchpad!

oh and one more ting — I discovered you can use Ableton Link to sync multiple devices wirelessly now?? like I could sync my laptop and my iPad running something else and they stay in perfect time. I didn't even know that existed before I left!!

Yeah, Ableton Link has been around for a while but they keep improving it. It's really powerful for live performance — you can sync with other musicians running Link-compatible apps on any device, no cables needed. Some DJs use it to sync with a second performer for b2b sets.

It works over WiFi so the only thing to watch out for is network stability in a club environment. Some performers bring a dedicated router just for Link. But for syncing your own devices it's rock solid.
trust me the noise is COMING!! got a set this weekend and I'm about to show people what Session View and the Launchpad can DO. thank you for listening to my whole DAW journey saga lol bless!!
Ha, the DAW saga has a happy ending! Kill it this weekend — go show them what a real live set looks like. Have fun out there!
